title: # Start Kernel Orchestration Start Kernel orchestration for the current task inside this Codex session. Execute immediately. Do not create or edit GitHub issues, pull requests, review comments, or issue comments. Do not inspect CI unless explicitly asked later. Backup-only GitHub Actions dispatch or...
summary_text: **High-confidence failure modes (local `/kernel` runtime canary path)** 1. **Live monitor and timeout audit enforce different phase contracts (real flake source).** - Live path enforces `required_phase_evidence` from config (`plan, simulate, critique, repair, replan`) via monitor/watch: [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L709), [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L368), config at [config.toml](/Users/masayuki/.codex/config.toml#L23). - Timeout fallback audit in canary script only requires `plan, simulate, replan`: [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L190). - This mismatch directly explains unstable behavior: live `/kernel` can timeout/miss ack, then pass via weaker fallback criteria. 2. **`source` filter mismatch in thread lookup can drop valid runs.** - Guard session resolver accepts `source IN ('cli','exec')`: [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L527), [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L544). - Timeout audit query uses only `source='exec'`: [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L174). - If runtime thread is recorded as `cli`, timeout audit reports failure even when guard would consider it valid. 3. **Bootstrap evidence validation is inconsistent between guard and timeout audit.** - Guard uses exact env path + strict trusted-root + `created_at` freshness: [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L63), [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L74), [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L87). - Timeout audit scans globbed files and uses filesystem `mtime` ±600s instead of payload `created_at`: [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L196), [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L212). - This creates nondeterministic pass/fail under concurrent runs, clock drift, or root override differences. 4. **`success_on_quorum` has timing-dependent behavior in watcher (ack path nondeterminism).** - On a line-read cycle, quorum returns success without checking required phases: [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L345). - On idle cycles, it does require phase evidence: [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L368). - Same run can pass/fail depending on log arrival timing, which is a correctness risk for ack stability. **Observed now:** running `RUN_CODEX_KERNEL_SMOKE=1 bash scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` in this workspace gave `/kernel` pass **via timeout audit** and `/k` pass in fresh session, which is consistent with the contract-split risk above.
